LAW AND MOTION 302, EFENDANTS TADICH GRILL, INC., AND MICHAEL BUICH'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION - DEFENDANTS TADICH GRILL, INC. AND MICHAEL BUICH'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR CONCEALMENT AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ICON INC. IS DENIED TO THE EXTENT THAT THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION SEEK DAMAGES AND OTHER RELIEF BASED ON ICON'S ROLE AS MANAGER OF THE SAN FRANCISCO RESTAURANT AND GRANTED TO THE EXTENT THAT THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION SEEK DAMAGES AND OTHER RELIEF NOT BASED ON ICON'S ROLE AS MANAGER OF THE SAN FRANCISCO RESTAURANT. SINCE ICON CONTENDS THAT ITS SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION ONLY SEEKS RELIEF BASED ON ITS ROLE AS MANAGER OF THE SAN FRANCISCO RESTAURANT, ICON CAN PROCEED WITH THOSE CLAIMS IN THIS COURT, BUT IN DOING SO ICON MAY NOT SEEK ANY RELIEF THAT IS NOT BASED ON ITS ROLE AS MANAGER OF THE SAN FRANCISCO RESTAURANT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185 =302/HK

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Cross-Complainants Fifth Cause Of Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-04-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling on CROSS-COMPLAINANT MICHAEL BUICH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Cross-Complainant's Fifth Cause Of Action is adopted as follows:

Cross-complainant Michael Buich's motion for summary adjudication on his fifth cause of action in his third amended cross-complaint is denied. Buich failed to maintain his burden of production per Code of Civil Procedure, 437c(p)(1). One of the elements of Buich's cause of action for breach of the personal guaranty is damages. Buich proffers a declaration and spreadsheet to establish damages. The spreadsheet is "based on records I received from Wells Fargo." (Buich Decl., Para. 10.) The Wells Fargo records were not supplied with the moving papers and there is no showing that they constitute admissible business records per Evidence Code, 1271. The court sustains Centioli's hearsay objection to the Buich declaration because his summary of damages is inadmissible hearsay. (See Pajaro Valley Water Mgmt. Agency v. McGrath (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 1093, 1106-1108 [reversing summary judgment where summary of damages was inadmissible hearsay].) Without that evidence, Buich fails to establish his prima facie cause of action for breach of the guaranty and does not meet his summary adjudication burden. His attempt to cure the deficiency in his reply papers comes too late. (See Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1537 ["The general rule of motion practice, which applies here, is that new evidence is not permitted with reply papers. This principle is most prominent in the context of summary judgment motions?."].)

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

DEFENDANT TADICH GRILL, INC., MICHAEL BUICH'S Motion For Leave To Amend.

Defendants and Cross-Complainants Tadich Grill, Inc. and Michael Buich's motion for leave to amend the Second Amended Cross-Complaint is granted. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. (See, e.g., Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428 ["The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified."].) If defendants wish to challenge the validity of the amended claims on limitations, waiver, or other grounds, they may do so by way of demurrer or motion for summary adjudicatoin. (See Brown v. Ralphs Grocery Co. (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 824, 843 ["The 'better course of action' is to allow a plaintiff to amend the [cross-]complaint 'and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings.'"].) Plaintiff and Cross-Defendants have not shown they would be prejudiced by allowing the amendment.

A Proposed Order is submitted by Email.
